HO-Azure :: attack-path-focused Azure recon
context :: tenant=auto subscription=auto output=table

[persistence] App Service persistence means a deployed web host plus deployment path plus configuration and identity plus a reachable entry path Azure will keep serving later, not a long-running shell on a VM.
App Service capability

╭────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬────────╮
│ action                                     │ api surface                                          │ status │
├────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼────────┤
│ create or reuse app service                │ Microsoft.Web/sites                                  │ yes    │
│ set or reuse deployment path               │ source control / package deployment / site config    │ yes    │
│ change app settings or identity attachment │ app settings / connection strings / managed identity │ yes    │
│ deploy or replace application code         │ zip deploy / package deploy / publish                │ yes    │
│ expose or reuse HTTP/HTTPS entry path      │ hostname / TLS / public network access               │ yes    │
╰────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴────────╯
This walkthrough shows the strongest currently visible App Service persistence path. The inventory below lists the other visible App Services without repeating the same narrative.
- Current identity can create a new App Service app or reuse this existing app.
   The App Service app is the Azure-side host that keeps the web workload deployed, configurable, identity-backed, and reachable over time.

- Current identity can set or reuse the deployment path this App Service will load.
   This is distinct from the application content itself: it is the trust path Azure uses to decide where code arrives from.
   Visible deployment signals here include repo github.com/contoso/customer-portal, branch main, GitHub Actions, continuous integration, run-from-package enabled.

- Current identity can change app settings and attach or reuse managed identity for this App Service.
   This is the execution-power layer for App Service: settings, connection strings, and workload identity shape what the deployed app can reach later.
   Visible config posture here includes 4 app setting(s), 2 Key Vault-backed setting(s), 1 sensitive-looking setting name(s), 2 connection string(s), 1 Key Vault-backed connection string(s), connection types Custom, SQLAzure.
   Visible sensitive-looking setting names include `DB_PASSWORD`.
   Current foothold `azurefox-lab-sp` already holds Owner at subscription scope.
   App Service identity `app-public-api-system` is visible here, but no Azure role-assignment rows are found for its principal ID.

- Current identity can deploy or replace the application code this App Service will run.
   This is the runnable content layer, separate from the deployment path that feeds it.
   Because the current identity already controls this App Service, package replacement, source-based deployment, or pipeline-backed redeploy are part of the defended App Service persistence path here.
   Run-from-package is already enabled.

- Current identity can expose or reuse this app's HTTP or HTTPS entry path so it remains reachable later.
   App Service re-entry is usually the app's reachable HTTP or HTTPS path, not a background process on a VM.
   Visible hostname here is `app-public-api.azurewebsites.net`.
   Visible entry posture includes public network access Enabled, HTTPS-only enabled, TLS 1.2.

- This App Service view stops at the main web host; use `persistence webjobs` when you need App Service WebJobs background-execution depth.

- Because the app stays deployed, reachable, and trusted until changed, this remains reusable App Service persistence even after the original session is gone.

Visible App Services
app service    | resource group | visible state                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | execution context                                                                    

app-empty-mi   | rg-apps        | Running; hostname app-empty-mi.azurewebsites.net; DOTNETCORE|8.0; run-from-package disabled; settings=2; conn=1; public Enabled                                                                                                   | `app-empty-mi-system` with Contributor at resource group `rg-apps`                   
app-public-api | rg-apps        | Running; hostname app-public-api.azurewebsites.net; NODE|20-lts; repo github.com/contoso/customer-portal, branch main, GitHub Actions, continuous integration, run-from-package enabled; settings=4; kv=2; conn=2; public Enabled | `app-public-api-system` with no Azure role-assignment rows found for its principal ID


Not collected by default
- the current command does not retrieve deployed application packages, repository contents, or source bundles, so operator intent is not inferred from code here
- the current command does not replay HTTP traffic or inspect runtime-side request handling, so conclusions stop at visible management-plane exposure posture
- the current command does not infer downstream API, data, or automation impact from runtime stack or setting names alone
- this App Service view stops at the main web host; use `persistence webjobs` when you need App Service WebJobs background-execution depth
